slices.focus: Hurricane anxiety management with a structured storm-week protocol
slices.summary: Facing a new round of hurricane threats, responded differently this time: maintained reflux treatment routine, limited forecast checking to fixed times, delegated public meetings to Miguel, and planned a five-item storm-week checklist (forecast window, food control, medication, pre-delegation, evening offline). Anxiety is still present but contained rather than consuming. Plans to share a simplified version of the plan with Lena and Miguel ahead of time.
open_loops: Whether the formal storm-week protocol will actually hold up when a real storm threat arrives
The specific alternative activity for evening offline time still needs to be defined beyond 'not checking weather'
Whether the delegation pattern (handing public meetings to Miguel) can be sustained across future storm seasons
decisions: Create a five-item storm-week checklist: forecast window, food control, medication adherence, pre-delegation, evening offline
Share a simplified version of the plan with Lena and Miguel ahead of time to avoid explaining during anxiety spikes
Use 'catching it early is part of doing well' as an internal self-talk anchor when warning signs appear
